mnemonics.title: O-mega-mnemonic
mnemonics.content: Think 'O' for 'Upper' (like 'O-ver'), 'Ω' (omega) is the last letter of Greek alphabet so think 'Ω' for 'Lower' (coming after O in alphabet order). Big O = Upper bound, Big Omega = Lower bound.

mnemonics.title: Theta Tightens the Bound
mnemonics.content: Big Theta Θ is like the '=' sign - it gives exact equality. Big O (∞ on side) gives 'greater than or equal to' upper bound. Big Omega (ω looks like 'w' for 'worse' or lower).

mnemonics.title: The Growth Rate Song
mnemonics.content: 1, log n, n, n log n, n², n³, 2ⁿ, n! - 'One Log N, N log N, N squared, N cubed, exponential then factorial' - sing it like a scale!

acronyms.expansion: Time function of input size n
Big O - Upper Bound (Order of magnitude)

acronyms.usage: Problem requires Ω(n log n) time to solve
Sorting takes Θ(n log n) time on average

acronyms.expansion: Little o - Strictly smaller upper bound
Little omega - Strictly larger lower bound

visualTips.visualization: Draw a curve f(n) and a horizontal line c×g(n) above it that eventually stays above f(n). Think 'ceiling' - O is the roof.
Draw a curve f(n) and a horizontal line c×g(n) below it that eventually stays below f(n). Think 'floor' - Ω is the foundation.
Draw f(n) sandwiched between two curves c₁×g(n) and c₂×g(n). Like a sandwich or sushi roll with g(n) as the rice and f(n) as the filling.
Draw multiple curves starting from origin: flat for O(1), slowly rising for log n, steeper for n, very steep for n², nearly vertical for 2ⁿ. Color code from green (good) to red (bad).

analogies.analogy: Speed limit sign - O(f(n)) is like saying 'your speed will never exceed this limit'.
Car's minimum speed capability - Ω(f(n)) is like saying 'this car can go at least this fast'.
Exact speed - Θ(f(n)) is like saying 'this car travels exactly at 60 mph'.
Compare to population growth - as city size increases, delivery time increases from constant (drone) to linear (walk) to quadratic (checking every house).
